tasks.spec:
You are a build worker on the OBE podcast repo. Your current working directory IS a git worktree of /mnt/d_drive/repos/podcast — edit files here directly. Do NOT git commit, branch, or push; leave changes uncommitted. Do not load skills or call MCP/Apps. No network access. Additive work only — never modify server/ or existing scripts.

CONTEXT: podcast outreach emails are being promoted from human-approved to AUTOSEND, gated on QA. The owner's canonical writing rules already exist as a machine validator in another repo; your job is to vendor it into this repo as a deterministic STYLE GATE the send path can call, plus negative tests proving the gate blocks style-violating drafts.

SOURCE MATERIAL (read-only, outside the worktree): /mnt/d_drive/repos/hubspot-daily-2/scripts/validate_email_draft.py (the validator: 17 codified checks — em-dash count 0, hyphen-as-dash, banned phrase categories incl. regex patterns, surveillance openers, signature 'Ankit' + '615.779.3629', one CTA, URL-on-own-line, length-by-warmth) and /mnt/d_drive/repos/hubspot-daily-2/reference/writing-guide.xml (the canonical rules XML it reads, synced from OB_mybcat).

OWNERSHIP: you own ONLY: scripts/style_gate/ (new dir: __init__.py, validate_email_draft.py vendored copy, writing-guide.xml vendored copy, gate.py, README.md) and tests/test_style_gate.py. Never modify anything else.

BUILD:
1. Vendor validate_email_draft.py + writing-guide.xml into scripts/style_gate/ VERBATIM except: add a provenance header comment naming the source path, the vendored date (2026-07-22), and the sync rule ('canonical lives in OB_mybcat → hubspot-daily-2; re-vendor on writing-guide revision change, do not hand-edit here'). If the validator hardcodes paths to its home repo, patch ONLY those path resolutions to resolve the vendored XML relative to its own location, and note each patched line in the README.
2. gate.py — thin wrapper: check_style(draft_text: str, warmth: str) -> (ok: bool, failures: list[str]) invoking the vendored validator logic in-process (import, not subprocess, if feasible; else subprocess on a temp file), plus a CLI: python3 scripts/style_gate/gate.py --file DRAFT --warmth warm; exit 0 pass, 1 fail printing every failure line.
3. tests/test_style_gate.py — pytest with embedded fixture drafts: (a) STYLE-POISONED drafts that must each FAIL with the named check: one with an em dash, one with banned phrases ('just checking in', 'hope this email finds you well', 'leverage'), one with two meeting links/CTAs, one missing the signature block; (b) one CLEAN compliant warm-followup draft (short, specific, signature 'Ankit' + '615.779.3629', one CTA link on its own line) that must PASS — iterate on the clean fixture until it genuinely passes the vendored validator; do NOT weaken the validator to make it pass. Assert failure output NAMES the violated check.

HOW TO RUN: PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 python3 -m pytest tests/test_style_gate.py -q

OUTPUT CONTRACT: leave changes uncommitted; write ./fix-summary.md ('# Fix Summary', '## Summary', '## Files Changed', '## Verification', '## Assumptions', <700 words) noting every validator line you had to patch for path resolution.
tasks.verified: pytest proved the vendored validator blocks all four style-poisoned drafts with named checks and passes a genuinely compliant draft; patch touches only scripts/style_gate and its test file

tasks.spec:
You are a fix worker on the OBE podcast repo. Your current working directory IS a git worktree of /mnt/d_drive/repos/podcast — edit files here directly. Do NOT git commit, branch, or push; leave changes uncommitted. Do not load skills or call MCP/Apps. No network access. HARD BOUNDARY: you are changing CARD METADATA GENERATION only — you must NOT send anything, NOT modify any executor, and NOT weaken any existing gate.

CONTEXT: today, scripts/obe_draft_to_linear_bridge.py (runs every 30 min via systemd) turns OBE Gmail outreach DRAFTS into Linear cards carrying an OPEN_ENGINE_GATE_PACKET (action class external_send); a separate executor sends only after a human replies APPROVE. The owner has promoted podcast outreach to autosend WHEN every QA gate passes. Design (do not deviate): the bridge computes a QA verdict per draft; ONLY when ALL checks pass does the card's gate packet gain "auto_approved_class": "podcast_outreach_qa_passed" plus a "qa_receipt" object. The executor is NOT in scope — a separate supervised change will teach it to honor that class; until then the marker is inert (cards still wait for human APPROVE), which makes this change safe to land.

READ FIRST (read-only): scripts/obe_draft_to_linear_bridge.py (whole file — match its conventions), CLAUDE.md sections on outreach conventions (referrer episode link rule, HUBSPOT_PREP_LINK vs discovery link, never 'nominated'), server/pipeline/outreach.py (template contracts, read-only), scripts/style_gate/gate.py IF present in your worktree (a concurrent lane is building it — see QA CHAIN below for how to depend on it safely).

OWNERSHIP: you own ONLY: scripts/obe_draft_to_linear_bridge.py and tests/test_bridge_qa_tagging.py. Never modify anything else.

BUILD — add a qa_chain(draft) function in the bridge that runs, in order, each returning (ok, reason):
1. suppression/eligibility: recipient not opted-out/suppressed per the bridge's existing data access (reuse what the bridge already loads; if it has no such signal, record check 'suppression' result 'unavailable' and treat as FAIL — fail closed, never invent access).
2. link integrity: every URL in the draft is on the allowlist derived from config (show URL, HUBSPOT_PREP_LINK, buildingempires.live episode links, transistor share links); any other URL = FAIL. A referral-intro draft (detected by the bridge's existing intro/referral pathway markers if present) without a buildingempires.live or transistor referrer episode link = FAIL.
3. placeholder scan: '{{', '}}', 'TODO', 'PLACEHOLDER', '[FirstName]'-style bracket tokens = FAIL.
4. style gate: import scripts.style_gate.gate check_style(draft_text, warmth='warm') — import lazily inside qa_chain and if the module is absent, record check 'style' result 'unavailable' and FAIL closed (the style lane lands in the same release).
5. caps precheck: read-only check against the bridge's existing send-cadence data if accessible; else 'unavailable' + note (executor still enforces caps at send time; this precheck failing 'unavailable' does NOT fail the chain — it is the ONLY non-fail-closed check, because caps are enforced downstream at send time; say this in a comment).
When ALL fail-closed checks pass: card packet gains auto_approved_class + qa_receipt {checks: [{name, ok, detail}], style_failures: [], validated_at ISO}. When ANY fails: NO marker, card body gains a 'QA: BLOCKED-FOR-AUTOSEND (reasons...)' line so a human sees why, and the card otherwise behaves exactly as today.

TESTS (tests/test_bridge_qa_tagging.py): self-contained; monkeypatch/fake the bridge's Gmail/Linear IO — the test must NEVER touch Gmail, Linear, or the network. Cover: all-pass → marker + receipt present; each fail-closed check failing alone → no marker + reason line; style-gate-absent → no marker (fail closed); non-allowlisted URL → no marker; unchanged card behavior for non-podcast/non-outreach drafts if the bridge distinguishes them (else note the assumption).

HOW TO RUN: PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 python3 -m pytest tests/test_bridge_qa_tagging.py -q

OUTPUT CONTRACT: leave changes uncommitted; write ./fix-summary.md ('# Fix Summary', '## Summary', '## Files Changed', '## Verification', '## Assumptions', <700 words). If the bridge's structure makes any required check impossible as specified, implement the closest fail-closed version and flag it prominently in fix-summary.md.
tasks.verified: pytest proved the QA chain fails closed on every gate (incl. style-gate-absent), only all-pass drafts get the inert auto_approved_class marker, and the patch touches only the bridge and its test

tasks.spec:
You are a read-only research worker on the OBE podcast repo. Your current working directory IS a git worktree of /mnt/d_drive/repos/podcast. You may create ONLY the two owned files below; treat everything else as strictly read-only. Do NOT git commit. Do not load skills or call MCP/Apps. No network access.

GOAL: the owner wants a complete per-guest/per-episode INFORMATION MANIFEST (headshot, links, bio, promo assets, 'etc') tracked in HubSpot as the central source of truth, with completeness, statuses, and notes visible per guest. Before any HubSpot property is created, the owner reviews a field-list proposal. Your job: derive that proposal FROM THE REPO'S ACTUAL ARTIFACTS, not from imagination.

INVESTIGATE (read-only; cite file:line for every field you propose): server/pipeline/post_prep.py and any asset-request lane code (what assets are requested from guests today); server/pipeline/content_generation.py + show_notes.py if present (what guest info content needs); episodes/ structure for 2-3 recent episodes (what per-episode files actually exist: headshots, bios, links, promo clips); docs/podcast-funnel-PLAYBOOK.md and docs/OBE-END-TO-END-FUNNEL-2026-07-10.md (what the funnel says each stage needs); CLAUDE.md (referrer episode link rule); server/pipeline/outreach.py (what guest fields outreach consumes); any Zernio/social scheduling code for promo asset requirements (image specs, clip formats).

OWNERSHIP: you own ONLY: docs/guest-manifest-field-proposal-2026-07-22.md and ./fix-summary.md (in the task dir).

OUTPUT — docs/guest-manifest-field-proposal-2026-07-22.md with EXACTLY these sections:
'# Guest Manifest Field Proposal (2026-07-22)'
'## Info manifest fields' — table: field | required/optional | source of truth today | evidence (file:line) | proposed HubSpot property name (prefix podcast_manifest_) | fallback asset if missing at publish.
'## Promo manifest fields' — same table shape for promotional items (episode artwork, clips, quote cards, social copy variants, publish URLs).
'## Statuses' — proposed per-field status vocabulary (missing / requested / chased-N / received / verified / fallback-used) and a per-guest rollup (podcast_manifest_completeness_pct, podcast_manifest_status).
'## Chasing policy observed today' — what the current asset-request lane actually does (cadence, channel), cited.
'## Open questions for Ankit' — anything the repo cannot answer (max 5, real questions only).
Every proposed field MUST carry at least one file:line citation from this repo; a field with no evidence goes under Open questions instead. Do not invent HubSpot property types beyond name suggestions.

HOW TO RUN (self-check): grep -c 'podcast_manifest_' docs/guest-manifest-field-proposal-2026-07-22.md should be >= 8, and every Info/Promo table row contains a ':' file citation.

OUTPUT CONTRACT: write ./fix-summary.md ('# Fix Summary', '## Summary', '## Files Changed', '## Verification', '## Assumptions', <700 words).
tasks.check: python3 '/home/ankit114/repos/ringer/templates/fix-swarm/checks/fix-swarm.py' --verify-command 'python3 -c "import re,sys; t=open(\"docs/guest-manifest-field-proposal-2026-07-22.md\").read(); missing=[s for s in [\"## Info manifest fields\",\"## Promo manifest fields\",\"## Statuses\",\"## Chasing policy observed today\",\"## Open questions\"] if s.lower() not in t.lower()]; props=len(re.findall(r\"podcast_manifest_\", t)); cites=len(re.findall(r\"[A-Za-z0-9_/.-]+\\.(?:py|md):\\d+\", t)); print(f\"missing={missing} props={props} cites={cites}\"); sys.exit(0 if not missing and props>=8 and cites>=10 else 1)"' --patch '/home/ankit114/ringer-work/podcast-loop-hardening-r3/manifest-fields-proposal.patch' --summary fix-summary.md --exported-summary '/home/ankit114/ringer-work/podcast-loop-hardening-r3/manifest-fields-proposal.summary.md' --owned-files 'docs/guest-manifest-field-proposal-2026-07-22.md'
tasks.verified: the proposal doc exists with all five sections, >=8 podcast_manifest_ property proposals, and >=10 file:line citations from the repo
